commits.msg: [Matching Engine] Availability scoring now logs whether user provided selected days before computing staff overlap to surface missing temporal context.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs user's hall location (if known) so engineers instantly see distance mismatches during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Goal relevance now logs user's stated problem (first 40 chars) so engineers instantly see what need drove the match during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Solution specificity now logs exhibitor's product count so engineers instantly see catalog breadth during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Actionability scoring now logs which concrete next-step mechanisms exhibitor offers so engineers see demo/meeting/sample availability instantly.
[Matching Engine] Intent confidence now logs whether user named a specific product category to reveal precision instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Intent confidence scoring now logs user query word count and question mark presence so engineers see signal clarity instantly.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now logs staff role types present on user's selected days so engineers see expertise alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now requires both hall coordinates AND user-selected days to award any credit — missing either zeroes the score.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now defaults to 0.075 (half weight) when structured data is missing so strong matches stay visible instead of vanishing.
[Matching Engine] Actionability scoring now requires exhibitor to offer at least one concrete next-step mechanism to earn any credit (zero for passive presence).
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's employee count so engineers instantly see scale mismatches during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Social proof scoring now logs reference customer count and outcome metrics so engineers see what earned trust signals during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs user's selected day count so engineers instantly see when temporal filter is missing during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Goal relevance now logs exhibitor's industry vertical tag so engineers instantly see sector mismatches during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Goal relevance scoring now requires exhibitor to name at least one concrete product/service capability to earn any credit (zero for pure PR).
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now awards 0.01 (half credit) when staff schedule is partial so incomplete data doesn't zero out strong matches.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs hall distance in meters so engineers see physical separation instantly during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Solution specificity now penalizes exhibitors listing >50 products as unfocused, rewarding catalog depth over breadth for sharper matches.
[Matching Engine] Solution specificity now logs exhibitor's product catalog size (count of distinct SKUs/offerings) to reveal generic vs. focused portfolios.
[Matching Engine] Actionability debug logs now show which concrete actions (demo/sample/meeting) earned credit so engineers see scoring logic instantly.
[Matching Engine] Goal relevance now logs exhibitor's top 3 solution keywords so engineers see semantic match quality instantly during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Intent confidence debug logs now show user's query word count and technical term density so engineers see specificity signals instantly.
[Matching Engine] Solution specificity scoring now awards zero when exhibitor presents only generic marketing claims without naming concrete products or features.
[Matching Engine] Intent confidence scoring now logs user's query clarity markers (question marks, specifics, vague terms) so engineers see why matches got downweighted.
[Matching Engine] Goal relevance debug logs now emit to stderr showing user's stated problem (first 60 chars) and exhibitor's solution claim for match transparency.
[Matching Engine] Actionability scoring now logs concrete actions offered (first 60 chars each) so engineers see what earned credit instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity now awards zero when user hasn't selected fair days so location boost requires temporal context to prevent false confidence.
[Matching Engine] Actionability score now requires exhibitor to list at least one concrete action (demo slot, sample pickup, meeting link) to earn any credit.
[Matching Engine] Availability now awards zero score when staff schedule is missing entirely so exhibitors must submit data to rank competitively.
[Matching Engine] Social proof now logs exhibitor's reference customer industries so engineers see domain-match quality instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs user's selected days count so engineers see temporal filter strength instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Social proof logs now show exhibitor's measurable outcomes (first 80 chars: percentages, time saved, cost reduction) so engineers see proof quality.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's budget tier (extracted from role/query) so engineers see price-point mismatch instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's employee count (exact number or range) extracted from role/profile so engineers see scale mismatch instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's target industry vertical (first 40 chars) extracted from query/profile so engineers see sector mismatch instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs exhibitor's actual industry vertical tags so engineers see why manufacturing buyers got matched to service providers.
[Matching Engine] Availability debug logs now show which of user's selected days overlap with exhibitor staff schedule so engineers see coverage gaps.
[Matching Engine] Social proof now awards half-credit (0.015) when exhibitor has named customers but no measurable outcomes so partial proof beats zero.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now falls back to 0.075 (half weight) when structured fields missing instead of zero so strong matches don't vanish from rankings.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit to stderr with [CFDBG] prefix so engineers can grep live rankings without filtering application noise.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now show inline computation when partial data present so engineers see degradation math in real time.
[Matching Engine] Availability now logs exhibitor's actual staff schedule (days/times present) so engineers see temporal coverage gaps instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now verifies user has selected fair days before awarding location bonus so walk-time estimates stay meaningful.
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now emit user's selected fair days so engineers see temporal scope when investigating zero-distance matches.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now awards half-credit (0.05) when hall data missing but user days present so temporal availability doesn't get ignored.
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now show exhibitor's hall coordinates so engineers see spatial data quality instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Social proof debug logs now emit exhibitor's actual reference customers (first 60 chars of names/industries) so engineers see proof instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now extracts buyer's company name from role/query text when missing from profile so exhibitors see who they're being matched to.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's actual budget constraint (exact currency amount or "unspecified") so engineers see price-gate failures instantly.

last_completed: [Matching Engine] Availability scoring now logs staff schedule array length so engineers instantly see coverage depth during live fair testing.
last_detail: Availability scoring now logs staff schedule array length so engineers instantly see coverage depth during live fair testing.
last_impact: When an exhibitor scores 0.01 (partial coverage) instead of 0.02 (full coverage), engineers currently cannot tell if the exhibitor provided schedules for 1 day vs. 4 days out of 5 user-selected days — all partial coverage looks identical. Logging schedule array length (e.g., `sched_days=4/5`) reveals whether missing data is a minor gap or a systemic absence, letting engineers prioritize exhibitor outreach to complete schedules that are 80% done vs. 20% done. This directly supports the CORE PRINC
next_target: Log proximity hall distance in meters so engineers instantly see when 'nearby' recommendations are actually across the venue.
